subject: FOIA Request – FY2024 Public Works Budget Line Items
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I am requesting a complete copy of the FY2024 Public Works departmental budget, broken down by line item, including any mid-year amendments approved by the Village Board. I am particularly interested in expenditures for road resurfacing, stormwater infrastructure, and contracted services. Please confirm receipt and provide the responsive documents within five business days as required by statute. I can accept electronic delivery in PDF format.

subject: FOIA Request – FY2024 Public Works Budget Line Items
body_clean: Ms. Carter, thank you for submitting your FOIA request. This confirms receipt as of today. We will respond within five business days as provided under 5 ILCS 140/3. The Village Clerk's office is assembling the responsive documents. You can expect the FY2024 Public Works budget detail and any Board-approved amendments in a single PDF package. If there are any items requiring legal review for exemption, we will notify you with a brief extension notice before the deadline.

subject: FOIA Request – FY2024 Public Works Budget Line Items
body_clean: Ms. Alvarez, I received the PDF package this afternoon and reviewed it. The road resurfacing line appears to reference a contract with Verdi Excavating. Could you also provide the executed contract document and any change orders issued this fiscal year? I believe those are separately responsive records not included in the budget summary. I am happy to submit a supplemental FOIA request if that is the required process.

subject: Records Request – Police Incident Reports, November 2024
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo, I live on Bohland Ave and there was a significant disturbance near my block in November that I'm still trying to get answers about. I'd like to request copies of any police incident reports filed between November 1 and November 30, 2024, for the 1600 block of Bohland Ave. I understand certain personal information may be redacted. Please let me know what form I need to complete or if this email is sufficient to start the process.

subject: Records Request – Police Incident Reports, November 2024
body_clean: Mr. Tibbs, thank you for reaching out. Your email constitutes a valid FOIA request under Illinois law — no separate form is required. I've forwarded your request to the Bellwood Police Department's FOIA officer, who handles law enforcement records. You should receive a response directly from their office within five business days. Personal identifying information on third parties will be redacted per 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c). Please don't hesitate to follow up here if you don't hear back by next Friday.

subject: FOIA – Village Board Meeting Minutes, January–March 2025
body_clean: Good afternoon. I've been following the Village Board's discussions about the proposed TIF district expansion and I'd like to review the official minutes from the January, February, and March 2025 regular meetings. I also want any attachments or exhibits entered into the record during those sessions. I'm a homeowner on Granville Ave and want to understand exactly what was discussed before any votes are taken. Thank you for your attention to this.

subject: FOIA – Village Board Meeting Minutes, January–March 2025
body_clean: Ms. Marchetti, thank you for writing. The January and February 2025 Board minutes have already been approved and posted to the Village website under the Clerk's page — I'm attaching direct links to save you time. The March minutes are in draft status pending Board approval at the April meeting; once approved they will be posted publicly and I can send them to you directly at that point. All exhibits referenced in those sessions are included in the posted agenda packets. Let me know if anything is missing.

subject: FOIA – TIF District #1 Annual Report and Increment Revenue 2023
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I am formally requesting the FY2023 Annual Report for Bellwood TIF District #1, including the total tax increment revenue collected, a breakdown of expenditures from the TIF fund, and the current projected surplus or deficit. Additionally, please provide any independent auditor's findings related to the TIF fund for FY2023. I believe these documents should be publicly available under the Illinois TIF Act and FOIA. Please confirm receipt.

subject: FOIA – TIF District #1 Annual Report and Increment Revenue 2023
body_clean: Ms. Carter, thank you — received. The FY2023 TIF District #1 Annual Report is a public document and I'm glad to get it to you. Our Finance Director is pulling the auditor's report as well; TIF fund expenditure details are included in that package. I'll have our office send you the full compilation electronically by end of week. If there are any items the Finance Director believes warrant a brief legal review before release, we'll flag that transparently and give you a timeline.

subject: Request for Copy of Verdi Excavating Contract
body_clean: To Mayor Okonkwo — I'm a taxpayer on Frederick Ave and I heard at the last ward meeting that the Village signed a pretty big contract with Verdi Excavating for drainage work this year. I'd like to see the actual contract — the scope, the dollar amount, the term. I'm not trying to cause trouble, I just want to know what we're spending money on. Is this something I can get through a FOIA request? Thank you.

subject: FOIA – Code Enforcement Violation History, 3100 Block Washington Blvd
body_clean: Hello, I'm in the process of purchasing a commercial property on the 3100 block of Washington Blvd and my attorney has advised me to request the code enforcement violation and inspection history for the parcel before closing. I'd like all notices of violation, citations, and inspection records on file going back five years. Please let me know the quickest way to get these. Time is somewhat sensitive due to our closing schedule.

subject: FOIA Request – Village Employee Salary Schedule FY2024
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I am a resident of Bellwood and I would like to request the current salary schedule for all Village employees for fiscal year 2024, including department heads and any employees earning above $75,000 annually. I understand this is public information under Illinois law. I am not requesting individual personnel files, only the compensation schedule. Please advise on the timeline for providing these records.

subject: FOIA – Taste of Bellwood Vendor Contracts and Revenue 2024
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo, I'm a journalism student at Triton College working on a piece about local summer festivals and their economic impact. I'd like to request copies of the vendor contracts for the 2024 Taste of Bellwood festival, the total application fees collected, and any revenue-sharing agreements with food or beverage vendors. I'm especially interested in whether any vendors received fee waivers and why. This is a formal FOIA request. Thanks so much for your help.

subject: Requesting Snow Removal Expenditure Records – Winter 2024-25
body_clean: Mayor, I've lived on Harvard Ave for over thirty years and I pay close attention to how the Village spends our money. Last winter felt like plowing response times were slower than usual and I want to know what we actually spent on snow removal operations for the 2024–2025 season — contractor costs, salt purchases, overtime, all of it. I believe this is public record. Please send me whatever documentation you have. I'd prefer email delivery.

subject: Requesting Snow Removal Expenditure Records – Winter 2024-25
body_clean: Mr. Grabowski, this is Frank DiMeo, Public Works Director. Mayor's office routed your request to me since this is operational data. For winter 2024–25: total salt expenditure was $48,200, overtime hours for plow crews ran about 340 hours above baseline, and we had one contracted assist from an outside vendor for a 72-hour event in January. I'm compiling the full cost breakdown as a spreadsheet. You'll have it by end of week. Response times citywide were within our target window for all events except the January 18 storm — that one stretched us.

subject: FOIA – Parking Enforcement Revenue and Citation Data 2025
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I am requesting records related to parking enforcement for calendar year 2025: total number of citations issued, revenue collected, number of citations dismissed or reduced on appeal, and any contracts with third-party collection agencies for unpaid fines. I live near the Metra station on St. Paul Ave and parking issues in that area are a constant source of frustration for residents. I want to understand the scope of enforcement activity. This is a formal FOIA request.

subject: Public Records Request – Stormwater Infrastructure Inspection Logs
body_clean: Hello Mayor's Office, my basement on Monroe St flooded twice this spring and my neighbor says this has been an ongoing issue in this area for years. I want to see whatever inspection records or maintenance logs the Village has for the storm sewer infrastructure between Eastern Ave and 25th Ave along Monroe. If there are work orders, complaints from other residents, or scheduled maintenance entries in the Village's system, I'd like all of it. Thank you.

subject: FOIA Follow-Up – TIF District Expenditures: Still Awaiting Auditor's Report
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I submitted a FOIA request in July 2025 for the FY2023 TIF District #1 auditor's report. Your office indicated it would be provided, but I have not received the document. It has now been nearly eleven months. This is well beyond any lawful extension period. I am formally renewing my request and asking for a written explanation of the delay. If the document is being withheld, please cite the specific exemption. I am prepared to file a complaint with the Public Access Counselor if I do not receive a substantive response within five business days.
